content.full_text:
Farn PoLrrrcAL PnecrrcEs CorraMrssroN
428 J Street. Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660. Fax (916) 322-0886
January 7,2003
Alan Seman, Councilmember
City of Rancho Mirage
69-825 Highway 111
Rancho Mirage, CA92270
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-02-330
Dear Mr. Seman:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the'Act,).1
QUESTTONS
You ask various questions with regard to the receipt of a campaign donation in
connection with your April 2002 city council committee, which akeady has been closed.
You ask the following:
1. May you deposit a donation recently received even though the election was in
April of 2002;
2. Are you required to re-open the committee to Re-Elect Alan Seman;
3. Are you required to deposit the donation, recently received, in the bank account
of the Committee to Re-Elect Alan Seman;
4. May the funds, recently received, be used to repay or reimburse Alan seman
for campaign expenditures of the committee to Re-Elect Alan Seman;
5. May the monies received from this donation be used for future political
expenditures;
6. Must you file the Form 460 with the FPPC stating deposit of this donation?
I Government Code sections 81000 - g1}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.


File No. A-02-330
Page No. 2
CONCLUSIONS
l. May you deposit a donation recently received even though the election was in
April of 2002? Yes.
2. Are you required to re-open the committee to Re-Elect Alan Seman? yes.
3. Are you required to deposit the donation in the bank account of the Committee
to Re-Elect Alan Seman? Yes.
4. May the funds, recently received, be used to rcpay or reimburse Alan Seman
for campaign expenditures of the Committee to Ri-Elect Alan Seman? No.
5. May the monies received from this donation be used for future political
expenditures? The funds may be used only for a political, legislatiie or
governmental purpose of the committee.
6. Must you file the form 460 with the FPPC stating deposit of this donation?
Yes.
ANALYSIS
You opened the Committee To Re-Elect Alan Seman for an April 2002 city
council election in the City of Rancho Mirage. Since the election you closed that
committee. Recently, after closing the committee, you received a tontribution and now
wish to use the funds.
As we have stated in the past, a campaign account must be reopened if a candidate
subsequently receives_contributions.' 1\fi'arierldrice Letter, 4-00-003.) Section g520f
requires that all contributions received by a candidate be deposited in a campai$ bank
account established for the candidate's election. If you re""ire contributiorrr totulirg
$1,000 or more in a calendar yeax after terminating your committee, you must reregister
as a corlmittee (and file a Form 410) andbegrn filing campaign disciosure reports (Form
460). If you receive contributions, but do not receive $1,OOO or more in a calJndar year,
you must file a Form 470, Officeholder and Candidate Campaign Statement--ShortForm,
for each calendar,year in which you receive contributio n". (Wirr"rAdvice Letter,.
supro.)
sections 85201 and,89512, among others, govern the use of campaign funds.
Section 85201 requires that:
"(c) All contributions or loans made to the candidate, to a person on
behalf of the candidate, or to the candidate,s controlled committee
2 We note, for the record, that you are a local officeholder. This analysis may not apply to a state
candidate.


File No. A-02-330
Page No. 3
shall be deposited in the account.
(d) AnV personal funds which will be utilized to promote the
election of the candidate shall be deposited in the account prior to
expenditure.
(e) All campaign expenditures shall be made from the account."
Therefore, if you intend to use personal funds to pay any outstanding expenses,
your personal funds must be placed into the campaign bank account prior to the funds
being expended.
Section 89512.5 requires that any expenditure by your committee must be related
to a "political, legislative, or governmental purpose of the committee." Moreover,
campaign expenditures from that account may only be made in connection with the
election to that office. This includes future political expenditures in connection with that
office. If however, you wish to use the funds for a different campaign or office, you
must follow the instructions above and open a separate bank account for the separate
campaign. Contributions that are not designated for one office or another maybe
allocated to any of the candidate's controlled committees pursuant to regulation 18523.
Section 89511.5 prevents you from reimbursing yourself for expenses you may
have paid from personal funds (copy attached).
If you have any other questions regarding this matter,please contact me at (916)
322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa
General
L
By:
Tocher
,Legal Division
Enclosure
CSrjg

C

l. May you deposit a donation recently received even though the election was in
April of 2002? Yes.
2. Are you required to re-open the committee to Re-Elect Alan Seman? yes.
3. Are you required to deposit the donation in the bank account of the Committee
to Re-Elect Alan Seman? Yes.
4. May the funds, recently received, be used to rcpay or reimburse Alan Seman
for campaign expenditures of the Committee to Ri-Elect Alan Seman? No.
5. May the monies received from this donation be used for future political
expenditures? The funds may be used only for a political, legislatiie or
governmental purpose of the committee.
6. Must you file the form 460 with the FPPC stating deposit of this donation?
Yes.
